title: Languas pricei
intro: Languas pricei, also known as the Lesser Galangal, is a type of rhizomatous herb belonging to the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are said to aid in digestive issues, reduce fever, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Languas pricei include gingerols, shogaols, and zingerone, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the Lesser Galangal is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in cooking and as a spice.
taxonomy: Languas pricei is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families in the world. Its classification as an orchid within the Orchidaceae family highlights its membership in a vast and fascinating group of flowering plants.
